about.intro:
Software engineering student at UiB with an uncommon background: before university, I managed real-time airspace operations as a specialist in the Royal Norwegian Air Force.

That environment — high stakes, zero tolerance for error, constant coordination under pressure — shaped a work ethic I now bring directly into software. I build carefully, communicate clearly, and take accountability seriously.
about.story:
On campus I hold real responsibility: I serve as Treasurer of the ECHO Sports Club, managing the club's finances and budget independently. As a board member of Hyggkom, I help organise the student environment across our faculty's reading rooms.

My technical passion sits at the intersection of systems and graphics. I'm currently building Arabat — a game project on GitHub that has pulled me deep into engine architecture, rendering pipelines, and performance-critical code.

I learn fast, build independently, and I don't wait for permission to figure things out.
about.aiNative: AI is part of my workflow, not a novelty. I use LLMs to write better code, debug faster, challenge my own architecture decisions, and compress learning curves. Developers who genuinely master working alongside AI will have a structural advantage — I'm building that fluency now, not later.

career.title: Operations Specialist (OR2)
career.company: Royal Norwegian Air Force — Ørland Air Station
career.description: Managed daily flight operations at one of Norway's primary F-35 bases. Coordinated incoming and departing aircraft, maintained operational logs, and led a team of conscripts as their NCO — responsible for readiness, inspections, and duty scheduling.

career.title: Conscript (OR1/OR1+)
career.company: Royal Norwegian Air Force — Bardufoss Airport
career.description: Operated the Wing Operations Centre in a safety-critical environment. Handled real-time flight requests, maintained precise logs, and allocated landing slots — all under time pressure with no margin for error.

career.title: Kindergarten Substitute
career.description: Supervised children independently across full shifts, managing daily routines, welfare, and safety with full accountability.

career.title: All-Sports Instructor
career.description: Solo instructor for groups of ~30 children aged 3–5. Designed and led structured physical sessions, adapting on the fly to group dynamics and energy levels.
